name: Role and Permission Management
description: Create and manage PostgreSQL roles with granular permissions for secure multi-user database access control.
prerequisites: Superuser or admin access to the database

steps.title: Create a new role with login
steps.description: Create a database user role with a password and login capability.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "CREATE ROLE app_user WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'secure_password' VALID UNTIL '2027-01-01';"
steps.tip: Always set VALID UNTIL for service accounts to enforce credential rotation.

steps.title: Grant database-level permissions
steps.description: Allow the role to connect to a specific database.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE mydb TO app_user;"

steps.title: Grant schema and table permissions
steps.description: Give the role read/write access to tables in a schema.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO app_user; GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO app_user;"

steps.title: Set default privileges for future tables
steps.description: Ensure the role automatically gets permissions on tables created in the future.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO app_user;"

steps.title: Create a read-only role
steps.description: Create a restricted role that can only read data.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "CREATE ROLE readonly_user WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'readonly_pass'; GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE mydb TO readonly_user; GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO readonly_user; GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO readonly_user;"

steps.title: List all roles and their attributes
steps.description: View existing roles with their permissions and settings.

faq.question: What is the difference between a role and a user?
faq.answer: In PostgreSQL, there is no difference. A role with LOGIN capability is effectively a user. Roles without LOGIN are used as groups that other roles can inherit from via GRANT.

faq.question: How do I revoke all privileges from a role?
faq.answer: Use REVOKE ALL PRIVILEGES ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public FROM role_name, then REVOKE CONNECT ON DATABASE dbname FROM role_name. Finally, DROP ROLE role_name if you want to remove it entirely.
